source_preflight.notes: 8 nodes (EMPTY, ZERO, NORMAL, BOUNDS, MAXVAL, PREDICT, RUN, COMPARE). All bold labels <= 4 words. Concept mapping: EMPTY -> §5.4 'Empty input' category + §5.1 '[]'; ZERO -> §5.4 'Zero' category + §5.2 'Minimum valid input | 0'; NORMAL -> §5.2 'Normal passing marks | mid values'; BOUNDS -> §5.1 '70, 80, 90 — each one deserves its own test' + §5.2 'Exactly the boundary between grades'; MAXVAL -> §5.4 'Minimum and maximum values' + §5.2 'Maximum valid input | 100'; PREDICT -> §5.5 step 3 'Write your prediction'; RUN -> §5.5 step 4 'Run the code'; COMPARE -> §5.5 step 5 'Compare the actual output to your prediction'. No scope-creep, no unexplained acronyms. Uses proven %%{init}%% syntax from topic 11.8 (same repo, same course week).

visual_checks.note: PNG not rendered in this environment (mmdc not available). Visual checks based on structural analysis of the .mmd source against the proven 11.8 side-by-side pattern. The %%{init}%% block, subgraph structure, nodeSpacing:55, rankSpacing:65, and classDef/:::suffix pattern are identical to the passing topic 11.8 diagram. BOUNDS dashed cross-edge to PREDICT mirrors GATE-->PATH_A/PATH_B pattern; label 'test each one' (3 words) is short enough to avoid edge_label_collision at 1400px width.

stats.layout: flowchart LR (two subgraphs: MAIN direction LR — input range zones; SIDE direction TB — test prediction steps)

color_assignment.ZERO: start (#a5d8ff) — edge case: zero / min value
color_assignment.NORMAL: auto (#d0bfff) — safe middle zone: no boundary hit
color_assignment.BOUNDS: hitl (#fff3bf) — critical check points: grade boundary values 70/80/90

findings.detail: 8-node side-by-side diagram. MAIN subgraph (LR) sequences the input range as a zone map: EMPTY (blue) -> ZERO (blue) -> NORMAL (purple, safe middle) -> BOUNDS (yellow, critical gate) -> MAXVAL (blue). SIDE subgraph (TB) shows the 3-step test prediction flow: PREDICT (blue) -> RUN (blue) -> COMPARE (green). Dashed cross-edge BOUNDS -> PREDICT labeled 'test each one' bridges the zone map to the procedure. Intent fully realized: normal range contrasted with edge-case zones, grade boundaries highlighted, test prediction steps attached.

All 8 nodes trace to topic 11.9 corpus sections only. 'Grade boundaries' correctly collapses 70/80/90 into one node (the subtitle 'exactly 70 / 80 / 90' expands it) without adding a node per boundary — stays within 8-node ceiling. 'Normal zone' uses corpus phrase 'normal passing marks' (§5.2). '> vs >= decides grade' subtitle traces to §5.3 off-by-one error examples. No week-12 or advanced concepts introduced.

BOUNDS uses :::hitl (yellow, stroke-width:3) — appropriate because grade boundaries are the critical human-attention checkpoints where the off-by-one bug hides; analogous to a gate requiring careful inspection. NORMAL uses :::auto (purple) — appropriate for the safe zone requiring no special test attention. COMPARE uses :::done (green) — terminal success state when prediction matches actual. No tier violations.

verdict: PASS (cycle 1). 8-node LR side-by-side. MAIN: zone map EMPTY(blue)->ZERO(blue)->NORMAL(purple)->BOUNDS(yellow)->MAXVAL(blue). SIDE: prediction flow PREDICT(blue)->RUN(blue)->COMPARE(green). Dashed BOUNDS->PREDICT bridge labeled 'test each one'. All labels <=4 words, all corpus-grounded (§5.1-5.5), no acronyms, correct color tiers, syntax matches proven 11.8 pattern. PNG render pending (mmdc not available in this environment — recommend running: mmdc -i artifacts/diagram.mmd -o artifacts/diagram.png -w 1400 -b transparent).
